info.title: Hermes Health API
info.description:
Medical records retrieval platform API.

## Getting started

A walkthrough from a clean account to a downloaded record deliverable. Most steps are API calls; the first three collect IDs and credentials from the web app.

### 1. Sign in

Open the Hermes Health web app and sign in.

### 2. Grab a sandbox project ID

Sandbox projects auto-complete patient searches and record requests against stub data, so you can exercise the full flow end-to-end without waiting on real claims. They are hidden on the projects page by default — toggle **Show Sandbox and Closed** to reveal them, open your sandbox project, and copy its ID from the URL (the segment after `/projects/`). You'll pass it as `<project_id>` on every subsequent route.

### 3. Mint your sandbox API key

Open **Settings** (top-right user menu, or navigate to `/settings`) and select the **API Settings** tab. The panel shows two keys: a production **API Key** and a separate **Sandbox API Key**. **Use the sandbox key for this walkthrough** — it is scoped to projects whose status is `Sandbox`, so test traffic can never read or mutate production data. If you don't see a sandbox key, generate one from the same panel. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer <your-sandbox-api-key>` on every request below; the same header shape applies to the production key when you later promote your integration to live projects.

### 4. Create a patient

Two routes accept the same `PatientForm` body — pick by ID strategy:

- `POST /v0/companies/{companyId}/projects/{projectId}/patients` — Hermes generates and returns the patient ID.
- `PUT /v0/companies/{companyId}/projects/{projectId}/patients/{patientId}` — you choose the ID. Re-PUT to upsert.

**If you already know which facility you want records from** (you have the hospital or clinic's name and address — you don't need a Hermes site ID, just the real-world identity of the place), you can skip straight to **step 7** and pass `siteName` / `siteAddressLine1` / etc. on the record-request body. Steps 5 and 6 exist to *discover* which facilities a patient visited, by running a claims search and browsing the resulting visits. Skip them if discovery isn't what you need.

Set `searchLevel` to `"SiteSonar"` to kick off claims-based discovery of where the patient has been seen. Omit it (or pass `"None"`) if you only want to register the patient — for example, when you already know the facility and are heading straight to step 7. `"PatientHistory"` layers diagnosis and treatment data on top of Site Sonar.

`zipCodes` is the list of ZIPs where the patient lived **at the time they visited each facility** — *not* their current ZIP. Pass every ZIP you know about; we use them to widen the claims search radius. The legacy single-string `zipCode` field is retained for backward compatibility — pass `zipCodes` in new code.

`startDateOfService` and `endDateOfService` bound the visit window the request covers. `authorizationExpirationDate` is the date the patient's signed authorization stops being valid for new releases. `capacity` describes who is legally signing the authorization — `SelfAuthorizing`, `Deceased`, `Minor`, `Comatose`, `CognitivelyImpaired`, `LegallyIncapacitated`, or `PowerOfAttorney`. All four are optional today, but a future revision of the API will make them required — populate them now so the future tightening is a no-op for your integration. The same body shape is accepted by both the POST and PUT routes above.

```json
{
  "firstName":                   "Jane",
  "lastName":                    "Doe",
  "dateOfBirth":                 "1990-04-12",
  "sex":                         "F",
  "zipCodes":                    ["94110", "02139"],
  "startDateOfService":          "2024-01-15",
  "endDateOfService":            "2024-02-20",
  "authorizationExpirationDate": "2027-01-15",
  "capacity":                    "SelfAuthorizing",
  "searchLevel":                 "SiteSonar"
}
```

### 5. Wait for the search to finish

Site Sonar runs asynchronously. You have two ways to be notified:

- **Subscribe to the `visits` webhook** (recommended) — see [Outbound webhooks](#outbound-webhooks) for the configuration UI and payload schema.
- **Poll** `GET .../patients/{patientId}` periodically and watch `searchStatus`. Values are `Inactive`, `Processing`, `Completed`, `Failed`, and `NoHit`. The parallel `siteSonarStatus` field is a deprecated alias kept for backward compatibility and will be removed — read `searchStatus` in new code.

Sandbox projects auto-complete the search within seconds, so you can advance the walkthrough without waiting on real claims data.

### 6. Read the visits

Visits land on `POST /v0/visits/browse`, which uses the typed query syntax described in [Browsing tables](#browsing-tables). The minimal query for "every visit for one patient on one project, sorted by earliest service date" is:

```json
{
  "where": {
    "patient": { "id": [{ "equals": "<patient_id>" }] },
    "project": { "id": [{ "equals": "<project_id>" }] }
  },
  "orderBy": [
    { "visit": { "earliestServiceDate": "asc" } }
  ]
}
```

Each row carries `visit`, `patient`, `site`, `payor`, `company`, and `project` blocks. Pick a visit and grab its `site.id` — that's what you'll feed into the record request below.

### 7. Create a record request

`POST /v0/companies/{companyId}/projects/{projectId}/patients/{patientId}/record-requests` takes a `RecordRequestForm`. Identify the facility one of two ways:

- **By site ID (preferred).** Pass `siteId` from a browse row — Hermes already knows the facility's address, NPI, and submission preferences.
- **By facility info.** If you don't have a site ID, pass `siteName`, `siteAddressLine1`, `siteAddressLine2` (optional), `siteCity`, `siteState`, and `siteZip`. Hermes will resolve the facility for you.

Toggle which deliverables you want with the booleans `medicalRecord`, `billing`, and `imaging`:

```json
{
  "siteId":        "<site_id>",
  "medicalRecord": true,
  "billing":       false,
  "imaging":       false
}
```

The response is a `RecordRequestMany { requests: [...] }` — one entry per request type you toggled on. Each entry's `request.id` is the ID you'll watch in the next step.

### 8. Wait for the request and download the deliverables

The same `RecordRequestOutput` payload carries both the status and the deliverable URLs, so once you have a copy with `request.status` set to `Completed`, the `deliverables` array on that same payload is what you download. Same shape as step 5:

- **Subscribe to the `requestStatus` webhook** — fires on every status transition. The body is a full `RecordRequestOutput`; when `request.status === "Completed"`, the `deliverables` array on that same body has the presigned URLs. No follow-up GET needed.
- **Poll** `GET .../record-requests/{recordRequestId}` and watch `request.status`. `Completed` is the success state; the same response includes `deliverables`, so the polling response *is* the download manifest. `AuthDenied`, `WrongSiteName`, `PatientNotFound`, `ServiceDatesNotFound`, `SiteClosed`, `Canceled`, `DuplicateRequest`, `FacilityRefusal`, and `NoRecordFound` are terminal failure states that won't produce a deliverable.

Each entry in `deliverables` is a `FileInfo` with a presigned `url` you can download directly — no `Authorization` header needed; the URL signs itself for about an hour. The sibling `deliverable` field on the same response is retained for backward compatibility from before we supported multi-deliverable requests, and will be removed. New integrations should iterate over `deliverables` and ignore `deliverable`.

Sandbox requests auto-complete to `Completed` shortly after creation with a stub deliverable, so you can verify the end-to-end download flow without contacting any real facility.

## Outbound webhooks

The backend delivers four per-company outbound webhooks — see the top-level `webhooks` section of this spec for the full schema of each payload:

- `requestStatus` — a record request was created or its status changed (or a sandbox request auto-completed).
- `visits` — a Veritas claims search completed (or a sandbox patient search auto-completed) and reported visit data for one or more patients.
- `requestNote` — a note was created on a record request.
- `patientNote` — a note was created on a patient.

All four POST JSON to the company-configured URL and sign the raw body with HMAC-SHA256 in the `X-Webhook-Signature` header. The shared secret is `companies.webhook_secret` (the UUID bytes — **not** the UUID's string representation). Consumers should respond with a 2xx status within 10 seconds; non-2xx responses are logged but not retried.

### Configuring webhook URLs and retrieving the secret

Webhook URLs and the shared signing secret are managed from the Hermes Health web app by any user with the `api-key` permission:

1. Sign in to Hermes Health.
2. Open **Settings** (top-right user menu, or navigate to `/settings`).
3. Select the **API Settings** tab.
4. In the **Webhooks** section, paste your endpoint URL into any of the four slots you want to consume — *Record Request*, *Visits*, *Record Request Note*, or *Patient Note*. An empty URL disables that webhook.
5. The **Webhook Secret** shown above the URL inputs is the HMAC shared secret — copy it once; the same UUID signs every outbound webhook for this company. If you ever need to rotate it, contact Hermes Health support.

## Embedding the auth-check UI

Hermes Health renders the auth-check analysis as a server-rendered UI that you can drop into your own product as an iframe. **This is the recommended integration path** for any surface that displays auth-check results to end users.

### Why embed?

The raw auth-check analysis schema (`Analysis`, `AuthCheck`, `CheckItem`, `AdditionalDocuments`, and friends) changes frequently — we ship updates as we expand coverage for new document types, new legal requirements, and new edge cases surfaced by production usage. Consumers that embed the iframe pick up every improvement automatically and never need to handle schema drift. Consumers that marshal the raw schema themselves accept ongoing maintenance cost.

Embedding also means you never have to forward your backend API key to an end-user browser — the embed token is scoped to a single PDF and expires in an hour.

### 1. Get an embed token

There are two ways to obtain a token. Both require an authenticated backend call with your bearer token.

**a. Eager field on patient / record-request responses.** Every `GET .../patients/<id>` and `GET .../record-requests/<id>` response includes a ready-to-use `embedToken` field when the authorization PDF is present. No extra round-trip is needed — this is the preferred path when you already fetch the patient or record request on page load.

**b. Explicit mint for the standalone flow.** For the standalone auth-checker, where the document is uploaded outside any patient context, `POST /v0/auth-check/{filename}/embed-token` accepts an `AuthCheckPatientData` body and returns an `EmbedTokenResponse { token }`.

Either way, the resulting token is a stateless, AES-256-CTR-encrypted string that carries the patient info, the PDF's S3 key, the minting company, and an expiry (one hour from mint). No database lookup happens at render time.

### 2. Drop the iframe into your page

Point an iframe at `GET /ui/auth-check/embed?token=<token>`:

```html
<iframe
  src="https://api.hermeshealth.com/ui/auth-check/embed?token=YOUR_TOKEN"
  width="100%"
  height="800"
  style="border: 0;"
  title="Auth-check analysis"></iframe>
```

The embed route is token-authed only — no session cookie is required — so it is safe to render inside a third-party site. The token may be passed to the end user.

### Pending-state behavior

If the analysis is still processing when the iframe loads, the embed renders a side-by-side view showing the PDF and a pending spinner, and automatically reloads itself (first poll after 45 seconds, then every 5 seconds) until analysis is ready. Consumers do not need to implement any polling loop of their own.

### Token lifetime

Embed tokens expire one hour after they were minted. `PatientOutput` and `RecordRequestOutput` mint fresh tokens on every fetch, so for session-style usage it's typically enough to read the token straight off the response and re-fetch on page navigation. For long-lived embeds, call `POST /v0/auth-check/{filename}/embed-token` again to rotate.

## Browsing tables

We expose a typed query syntax for large tabular resources. Today this covers `POST /v0/visits/browse`, `POST /v0/procedures/browse`, and `POST /v0/diagnoses/browse`, and we'll move additional list endpoints onto the same contract over time. Every browse route accepts the same JSON request shape — column selection, filters, sorts, aggregates, search, and pagination — and returns a `{ table, aggregate, total }` envelope.

Each route registers its own request/response schemas in `components.schemas` as `{Entity}BrowseRequest` and `{Entity}BrowseResponse` (e.g. `VisitBrowseRequest`, `ProcedureBrowseResponse`), along with per-slot types (`{Entity}BrowseFilter`, `{Entity}BrowseSort`, `{Entity}BrowseSelect`, `{Entity}BrowseRow`, `{Entity}BrowseAggregateRequest`, `{Entity}BrowseAggregate`). Those schemas are the authoritative list of which columns each route accepts; this section documents the shared syntax so each per-route schema doesn't have to repeat it.

### Root request shape

```json
{
  "select":    { },
  "where":     { },
  "orderBy":   [ ],
  "aggregate": { },
  "search":    "...",
  "take":      100,
  "skip":      0
}
```

Every top-level field is optional:

- Omit `select` to return every column for every joined entity.
- Omit `where` to return every row the caller is allowed to see.
- Omit `orderBy` to use the route's default sort (typically created-at).
- Omit `aggregate` to skip roll-up computation.
- Omit `search` to skip the cross-column substring match.
- Omit `take` to return every matching row (useful for exports).
- Omit `skip` to start at the first row.

### Entity slots and joins

A browse response is a flat table of rows, but each row is keyed by entity: visits join through to patient, site, payor, company, and project; procedures join through to visit, patient, site, and so on. Every slot of the request — `select`, `where`, `orderBy`, `aggregate` — mirrors this join structure and is keyed by entity name (camelCase). The root entity uses its own name (`visit`, `procedure`, `diagnosis`); joined-in entities use their own keys.

The exact set of entities and columns per route is documented by the corresponding `{Entity}BrowseRequest` schema — inspect it to see which keys each slot accepts.

### Toggling columns with `select`

`select` is an entity-keyed object where each inner block maps column name to a boolean:

```json
{
  "select": {
    "visit":   { "id": true, "earliestServiceDate": true },
    "patient": { "firstName": true, "lastName": true }
  }
}
```

Every column defaults to `false` inside a `select` block. Omitting an entity's block entirely returns every column for that entity; omitting the top-level `select` returns every column for every entity. Response rows mirror the request — each selected entity appears as a nested object keyed by entity name, with only the selected columns present. Unselected columns are absent from the JSON (not `null`).

### Filtering with `where`

Each entity block under `where` maps a column name to a **list of predicates**. All predicates — across columns, across entities, and across entries within the same list — are **ANDed** together.

Every predicate is one of:

- **Value predicate** — an object with a single operator key. The available operators depend on the column's type:
  - Text (`string`): `equals`, `notEquals`, `contains`, `startsWith`, `endsWith`
  - Ordered (`integer`, `decimal`, `date`, `datetime`, `double`): `equals`, `notEquals`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`
  - Categorical (`bool`): `equals`, `notEquals`
- **List predicate** — an object with `in` or `notIn` whose value is an array of candidate values. Available on every column type.
- **Null predicate** — the literal string `"isNull"` or `"isNotNull"`. Available on every nullable column.

```json
{
  "where": {
    "visit": {
      "earliestServiceDate": [
        { "gte": "2025-01-01" },
        { "lt":  "2026-01-01" }
      ]
    },
    "patient": {
      "lastName": [ { "startsWith": "Smi" } ]
    },
    "site": {
      "state":      [ { "in": ["CA", "NY", "TX"] } ],
      "systemName": [ "isNotNull" ]
    }
  }
}
```

The generated `{Entity}BrowseFilter` schema encodes the valid operator set per column, so requests with mismatched operators (e.g. `contains` on an integer) fail deserialization with a 400.

### Sorting with `orderBy`

`orderBy` is an **array**, applied in order — the first entry is the primary sort, the second breaks ties, and so on. Each entry picks one entity and one column:

```json
{
  "orderBy": [
    { "visit":   { "earliestServiceDate": "desc" } },
    { "patient": { "lastName": "asc" } }
  ]
}
```

Direction is `"asc"` or `"desc"`. A stable per-row tiebreaker (typically the row's `id`) is appended automatically, so pagination is deterministic even when the requested sort produces ties. Omit `orderBy` entirely to use the route's default sort.

### Aggregates

Pass an `aggregate` block to compute roll-ups over the **filtered** row set — pagination (`take` / `skip`) does **not** narrow the aggregated rows, so aggregates always describe the full filtered population. Every entity exposes `count`; entities with numeric columns also expose `sum`, `avg`, `min`, and `max`, each keyed by column name with boolean opt-ins.

```json
{
  "aggregate": {
    "visit": {
      "count": true,
      "min":   { "earliestServiceDate": true },
      "max":   { "latestServiceDate":   true }
    }
  }
}
```

Results come back on `response.aggregate` in the same shape. Omit the block (or set every opt-in to `false`) to skip aggregation.

### Full-text `search`

The top-level `search` string applies a case-insensitive substring match across every text-like column on every joined entity. It is ANDed with `where`. Use it to power a single-box search UX; use `where` for precise queries.

### Pagination

`skip` and `take` both count **matching rows** (post-filter, post-search). Responses include `total` — the count of matching rows before pagination — so clients can render "page X of N" without a second request. Omit `take` to stream every matching row; combine that with `Accept: text/csv` to export.

### Response

```json
{
  "table":     [ ],
  "aggregate": { },
  "total":     4217
}
```

- `table` is the paginated rows, each mirroring the `select` shape.
- `aggregate` mirrors the request's `aggregate` shape; absent when no aggregates were requested.
- `total` is the count of rows matching `where` + `search`, before `skip` / `take`.

### CSV export

Every browse route content-negotiates on the `Accept` header. `application/json` (the default) returns the envelope above; `text/csv` returns a CSV of the selected columns only — `aggregate` and `total` are omitted from CSV output. Pair `Accept: text/csv` with an absent `take` to stream a full export.

get.summary:
Fetch the analysis payload and presigned URLs for a standalone auth-check
upload.
get.description:
The returned `FileResponse.analysis` wraps the raw `Analysis` / `AuthCheck`
schema, which evolves as we expand document-type and edge-case coverage.
If you are rendering the result to end users, prefer the iframe embed over
consuming this schema directly — see
[Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui).

This endpoint also returns a presigned `uploadUrl`. That is preserved for
backwards compatibility — new integrations should use `PUT
/v0/auth-check/<filename>` to obtain an upload URL, which avoids the extra
S3 read-side work this endpoint performs.

put.summary: Request a presigned S3 upload URL for a standalone auth-check file.
put.description:
Use this endpoint whenever you need to upload — previously customers had to
call `GET /v0/auth-check/<filename>` and pluck the `uploadUrl` out of a
combined read/write response. Prefer this route: it never touches S3 on
the read path, so it is cheaper and returns faster.

After a successful `PUT` to the returned `uploadUrl`, call
`GET /v0/auth-check/<filename>` to fetch the analysis payload and a
presigned download URL for the uploaded object.

post.summary: Mint an embed token for iframing the auth-check UI.
post.description:
The token encapsulates the patient info and PDF storage key so the
read-only embed view at `GET /ui/auth-check/embed?token=...` can render
without any session or DB lookup. Tokens are stateless, AES-256-CTR
encrypted, and expire one hour after minting.

This endpoint is for the **standalone** flow (a document uploaded
outside any patient context — typically via the auth-checker UI). For
patient- or record-request-scoped documents, `PatientOutput` and
`RecordRequestOutput` responses include a pre-minted `embedToken` field
and this extra round-trip is unnecessary.

See [Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui) for the
full iframe integration guide.

400.description: Bad request (patient has record requests — delete those first)

get.summary: Fetch the authorization file plus its auth-check analysis for a patient.
get.description:
The `analysis` field inside the response wraps the raw `Analysis` /
`AuthCheck` schema, which changes often as we expand document-type and
edge-case coverage. For end-user-facing surfaces, prefer the iframe embed
via the `embedToken` on `PatientOutput` — see
[Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui).

patch.summary: Update patient authorization info (dates, sex, authorization type, capacity, SSN)

post.summary: Mark a newly uploaded patient authorization as needing approval.

get.summary:
Get authorization analysis for a specific record request.
Uses request-level date overrides (if set) instead of patient-level dates.
get.description:
The `analysis` field inside the response wraps the raw `Analysis` /
`AuthCheck` schema, which changes often as we expand document-type and
edge-case coverage. For end-user-facing surfaces, prefer the iframe embed
via the `embedToken` on `RecordRequestOutput` — see
[Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui).

400.description: Bad request (e.g. PatientHistory on InsuranceLife project)

post.summary:
Atomically claim the next unowned patient and assign the caller as the
owner. Body schema mirrors `POST /v0/patients/browse`: pass any
combination of `select` / `where` / `orderBy` / `search` to narrow which
patients are eligible (e.g. set `where.company.id = { equals: 7 }` to
scope to a single company). The route always additionally requires
`ownerUserId == null`. Sort + pagination apply normally; the first row
is claimed. Publishes a `PatientPatched` event setting `ownerUserId` to
the caller and returns the full `PatientOutput` for the claimed patient.

404.description: No unowned patient matches the supplied filter.

post.summary: Start a site-finder job to resolve a site by name, address, and optional identifiers.
post.description:
Runs the site resolution pipeline (geocoding, embedding search, AI matching)
in the background and returns a `jobId`. Poll `GET /v0/site-finder/jobs/<job_id>`
until `status` is `Completed` or `Failed` to retrieve the result.

get.summary: Fetch the status and result of a site-finder job started via `POST /v0/site-finder`.
get.description:
Poll this endpoint until `status` is `Completed` or `Failed`. On completion,
`siteFinder` contains the resolution result and `siteFinderProposal` contains
a signed proposal to create a new site if no match was found.

post.description:
Supports two creation paths:

- **Token path** (from site-finder): when `siteFinderToken` is present, the request
  originated from the site-finder pipeline (`POST /v0/site-finder`, polled via
  `GET /v0/site-finder/jobs/<job_id>`). The token is an HMAC-signed blob containing
  pre-geocoded data (address, coordinates, place ID, etc.) produced during site
  resolution. The server verifies the HMAC signature and extracts the trusted
  geocoded fields, skipping the live geocoding step. This avoids redundant geocoding
  and ensures the created site matches exactly what site-finder resolved.

- **Standard path** (manual): without the token, the server geocodes the provided
  address from scratch using the geocoding service.

400.description: Bad request (invalid token, geocoding failure, etc.)

get.summary: Retrieve a single site by ID with its departments, Google Maps URL, and embedding status.

post.summary: Trigger AI research for a site in the background.
post.description:
Returns the current site data immediately. The research runs asynchronously;
poll `GET /v0/sites/<site_id>` and check `researchFinished` for completion.

Analysis.description:
On-disk shape of the sidecar's `"analysis"` key — what the upload-processor
lambda writes. The bundle returned to routes/renderers extends this with
overlay state (corrections, audited) that lives alongside `"analysis"` at
the sidecar root.

This schema changes frequently as we expand document-type coverage, legal
requirements, and edge cases. If you are rendering the result to end users,
prefer the iframe embed over consuming this schema directly — see
[Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui).

ApiErrorBody.description: Error body returned by all error responses.

AuthCheck.description:
Authorization form analysis result.

This schema changes frequently as we expand document-type coverage, legal
requirements, and edge cases (additional document types, sensitive-info
handling, minor/deceased representative rules, etc.). Consumers that marshal
this payload themselves accept ongoing maintenance cost.

For any surface that displays auth-check results to end users, prefer the
iframe embed over consuming this schema directly — see
[Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui).

fullName.description: The full name of the patient

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the patient

mobile.description: The mobile phone of the patient

socialSecurityNumber.description: The Social Security number of the patient

email.description: The email address of the patient

oneOf.description: The end date of service (may be a concrete date or "Present")
The authorization expiration (may be a date or an event description)
Sensitive information authorization requests extracted from the form

disclosingEntityName.description: Name of the entity disclosing PHI

authorizedRecipientInformation.description: Company/firm information for the authorized recipient

authorizationExecution.description: Signature, date, and printed name from the authorization form

oneOf.description: Additional supporting documents (POA, birth cert, death cert, etc.)

phiDescription.description: Description of PHI to be disclosed

purposeOfDisclosure.description: Reason for disclosure (insurance, legal case, personal use, etc.)

potentialForRedisclosure.description: Statement that disclosed PHI might be redisclosed

patientRightsAcknowledgement.description: Statement that treatment is not conditioned on signing

oneOf.description:
On the bundle (not a separate projection) so the renderer can ask
which fields the delivery team touched in the same in-memory pass.

AuthCheckBundle.description:
Wraps the on-disk `Analysis` with the delivery-team overlay state held
next to it in the sidecar. `#[serde(flatten)]` keeps Analysis's fields
flat at the top level so consumers see one bag, not nested. The overlay
fields are stripped before the bundle reaches users without
`AuditAuthChecker`.

AuthCheckPatientData.description:
Lightweight struct with only the patient fields needed by `Analysis::new` for authorization checks.
Used instead of `PatientResponse` so callers (like the standalone auth checker) don't need
to construct a full patient record.

dateOfSignature.description: The date of signature of the requester

printedName.description: The printed name of the requester (not necessarily the patient)

AuthorizationExpiration.description:
Authorization expiration extracted from the form.
May be a concrete date or an event description (e.g., "until legal matter concludes").

AuthorizationStatus.description:
Authorization validation status shared between Site Sonar and Patient History.
This represents the state of the patient's authorization validation.

oneOf.description: The mailing address of the company/firm

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the company/firm

email.description: The email address of the company/firm

childName.description: The name of the child on the birth certificate

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the child on the birth certificate

motherName.description: The name of the mother on the birth certificate

fatherName.description: The name of the father on the birth certificate

certificateNumber.description: The certificate number of the birth certificate

issuanceDate.description: The date of issuance of the birth certificate

isVoid.description: Is the document marked as VOID, CANCELLED, or REVOKED?

status.description: Status of the checklist item: passed (green), failed (red), or warning (yellow)

message.description: Specific findings about what was found or missing

courtName.description: The name of the court on the court order

caseNumber.description: The number of the case on the court order

dateOfOrder.description: The date of the court order

judgeName.description: The name of the judge on the court order

partiesInvolved.description: The parties involved in the court order

specificDirections.description: What the court ordered regarding records

isVoid.description: Is the document marked as VOID, CANCELLED, or REVOKED?

email.description:
Optional email to impersonate another user when creating the note.
Requires ImpersonateUser permission and the target user must be in the same company.

decedentName.description: The name of the decedent on the death certificate

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the decedent on the death certificate

dateOfDeath.description: The date of death of the decedent on the death certificate

placeOfDeath.description: The place of death of the decedent on the death certificate

certificateNumber.description: The certificate number of the death certificate

issuanceDate.description: The date of issuance of the death certificate

informantName.description: The name of the informant (person who reported the death, often next of kin)

nextOfKinNames.description: The next of kin listed on the death certificate. A certificate may list multiple surviving relatives.

survivingSpouse.description: The surviving spouse listed on the death certificate

isVoid.description: Is the document marked as VOID, CANCELLED, or REVOKED?

holderName.description: The name of the license holder

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth on the license

stateOfIssuance.description: The state that issued the license

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the license

isVoid.description: Is the document marked as VOID, CANCELLED, or REVOKED?

EmbedTokenResponse.description: Response returned by the embed-token minting endpoint.

FileResponse.description:
Unified file response covering both read (downloadUrl, analysis, loose files)
and write (uploadUrl, uploadHeaders) halves. Fields are populated based on
the S3 helper invoked: `presigned_read` leaves the write fields empty,
`presigned_write` leaves the read fields empty, and `presigned_read_write`
populates both.

wardName.description: The name of the ward (protected person)

guardianNames.description: The names of the guardians. Guardianship orders may appoint co-guardians.

wardDateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the ward

courtName.description: The name of the court on the guardianship papers

caseNumber.description: The number of the case on the guardianship papers

dateGranted.description: The date the guardianship was granted

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the guardianship

isVoid.description: Is the document marked as VOID, CANCELLED, or REVOKED?

decedentName.description: The name of the decedent (deceased person)

administratorNames.description: The names of the administrators (persons appointed by the court when there is no will). The court may appoint co-administrators.

courtName.description: The name of the court that issued the letters

dateIssued.description: The date the letters were issued

isVoid.description: Is the document marked as VOID, CANCELLED, or REVOKED?

decedentName.description: The name of the decedent (deceased person)

executorNames.description: The names of the executors (persons granted authority under the will). A will may name co-executors.

courtName.description: The name of the court that issued the letters

dateIssued.description: The date the letters were issued

isVoid.description: Is the document marked as VOID, CANCELLED, or REVOKED?

MatchingTuningForm.description:
Form version of matching tuning — all fields optional.
Deserialized from frontend requests; merged with `MatchingTuning::default()`.

siteFinderToken.description: When present, this request came from a site-finder proposal.

decedentName.description: The name of the decedent as stated on the affidavit

dateOfDeath.description: The date of death as stated on the affidavit

affiantName.description: The name of the affiant (person swearing the affidavit, typically the claimed next of kin)

affiantRelationship.description: The affiant's stated relationship to the decedent (e.g. spouse, child, parent, sibling)

nextOfKinNames.description: The next of kin named on the affidavit. Often just the affiant, but some affidavits enumerate multiple heirs.

swornDate.description: The date the affidavit was sworn or signed

isNotarized.description: Whether the affidavit bears a notary seal, notary block, or commission expiration

isVoid.description: Is the document marked as VOID, CANCELLED, or REVOKED?

Npi.description:
A validated National Provider Identifier.

Valid values are 1–9,999,999,999 (up to 10 digits).
[`Display`] pads to 10 digits with leading zeros.
[`Serialize`] emits a zero-padded decimal string.
[`Deserialize`] accepts either a JSON number or a numeric string.

PatientAuthorizationInfoForm.description: Form for updating patient authorization info (dates, sex, authorization type, capacity, SSN)

PatientCapacity.description:
Describes the patient's capacity to authorize release of their own medical records.
When a patient cannot self-authorize, a personal representative, guardian, or
power of attorney holder must sign the authorization on their behalf.

PatientFieldPatch.description: Mirrors the `SitePatch` enum pattern for single-field patient updates.

patientHistoryAvailable.description:
Whether patient history feature is available for this patient.
False when project purpose is InsuranceLife.

createdByEmail.description: Email of the user who created this patient.

ownerEmail.description:
Email of the user currently assigned as the owner. None when the
patient is unowned or when the owner record can no longer be
resolved.

embedToken.description:
Pre-minted embed token for iframing a read-only auth-check view.

Pass the token to `GET /ui/auth-check/embed?token=...` to render the
analysis UI inside your own page without proxying the raw analysis
schema. `None` when the authorization PDF has not yet been uploaded.
Expiry is one hour from fetch time — re-fetch the patient for a fresh
token. See [Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui).

PatientResponse.description:
Response struct for single patient endpoints. Includes `site_sonar_status` for backwards
compatibility, which is a deprecated alias for `search_status`.
Use this for `get_patient` responses; use `Patient` for dynamic queries in `get_patients`.

zipCode.description: Backward compat: first zip code (if any).

zipCodes.description: All zip codes for locations where the patient received care.

siteSonarStatus.description:
Backwards compatibility alias for `search_status`.
Deprecated: use `search_status` instead.

ownerUserId.description:
Assignee for queue-based work distribution. Distinct from `user_id`,
which is the creator. Cleared/set via the patient queue endpoint.

PatientSearchLevelForm.description: Form for updating patient search level

PatientWebhookPayload.description: A patient notification payload (notifies that patient data is ready)

principalName.description: The name of the principal (person granting power)

agentNames.description: The names of the agents (persons receiving power). A POA may name multiple co-agents.

poaType.description: The type of power of attorney

effectiveDate.description: The effective date of the power of attorney

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the power of attorney

attestationType.description: The method of attestation of the power of attorney

isVoid.description: Is the document marked as VOID, CANCELLED, or REVOKED?

ProjectPatch.description: One-field-at-a-time patch for the project PATCH endpoint.

oneOf.description:
Primary delivery line (street number + street, or PO Box line). For
Puerto Rico addresses with an urbanization, put `URB <name>` on this
line. Leave `siteAddressLine2` for suite / unit / apartment.

Optional secondary designator (suite, unit, apartment, floor). Empty
and whitespace-only strings are coerced to `null`.

**Backward compatibility only.** Retained so existing external
integrations that send a single `siteAddress` string keep working.
New integrations should send `siteAddressLine1` / `siteAddressLine2`
instead — if either of those is provided, this field is ignored.

You *may* pack an entire address into `siteAddressLine1` and we will
attempt to resolve it, but resolution will be less accurate than a
properly structured two-line address. Puerto Rico urbanization in
particular is not reliably parseable from a single line.

patientAuthorization.description:
Patient-level authorization file. Used as a fallback when the request
doesn't have its own authorization. Exposing both lets the UI disable
"Generate Packet" when neither is present.

oneOf.description:
Default delivery method to use when the user hasn't picked an override.
Computed from the department and site — see `recommend_delivery_method`.

embedToken.description:
Pre-minted embed token for iframing a read-only auth-check view.

Pass the token to `GET /ui/auth-check/embed?token=...` to render the
analysis UI inside your own page without proxying the raw analysis
schema. `None` when the authorization PDF has not yet been uploaded.
Expiry is one hour from fetch time — re-fetch the record request for a
fresh token. See [Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui).

oneOf.description: Assigns the owner/assignee of the request. Pass `null` to clear.

owner.description: Assigns the owner/assignee of the request. Pass `null` to clear.

submittedSiteAddress.description:
Legacy single-line address. Readers should prefer
`submittedSiteAddressLine1` / `submittedSiteAddressLine2` when
either is set; this field is retained for existing rows that
predate HER-1206 and for the `siteAddress` backward-compat surface
on `POST /record-requests`.

phone.description: Phone number to call to submit a revocation, as written on the form

fax.description: Fax number to submit a revocation, as written on the form

email.description: Email address to submit a revocation

oneOf.description: Mailing address to submit a revocation (any subset of fields may be present)

RightToRevoke.description:
LLM-extracted right-to-revoke block. Both subfields nullable — the backend
decides pass/warn/fail based on what's present.

contactInfo.description: Contact channels the form specifies for submitting a revocation

SearchLevel.description:
What level of search was requested for the patient.
PatientHistory implies SiteSonar (you need WHERE before WHAT).

SearchStatus.description: Unified processing status for search data retrieval.

status.description:
Status of the sensitive information request: not requested, requested without patient
authorization, or requested with patient authorization

message.description: Specific findings about what was found or missing

ServiceEndDate.description:
End date of service extracted from the authorization form.
May be a concrete date or "Present" (meaning ongoing/current).

SiteFinderProposal.description:
Proposal returned when site-finder finds no match.
Contains all fields that would be inserted plus an HMAC token.
The client submits this blob to POST /v0/sites to create the site.

siteFinderToken.description: HMAC token signing all fields above. Omitted when reconstructing for verification.

SiteResolutionInput.description:
Input for the unified site resolution pipeline.

Used by both the user-facing site finder and batch ingestion callers.
Construct via `SiteResolutionInput::new(...)` which takes the required
identity fields; everything else defaults to `None`/`false`.

addressLine2.description:
Optional secondary address line — suite/unit/apt, or for Puerto Rico
addresses the `URB <name>` urbanization line per USPS Publication 28.

authoritativeRoi.description:
When true, `medical_roi_id` is treated as a stable unique identifier
for shortcut matching (skips geocoding/AI).

autoResearch.description:
When true, automatically trigger background site research after resolution.
Always forced to `false` by the user-facing `/v0/site-finder` endpoint.

UsAddressForm.description:
Partial/form US address — all fields optional (for inputs, DB rows, patches,
and LLM-extracted addresses where any component may be missing).

VisitWebhookPayload.description:
Root payload for the outbound visits webhook. Delivered to
`companies.visits_webhook` when a claims search completes with visits
discovered for one or more patients.

tags.name: patient-authorization
tags.description: Patient authorization. Endpoints that return the analysis schema are marked as such; if you display auth-check results to end users, prefer the iframe embed described in [Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui).

tags.name: record-request-authorization
tags.description: Record request authorization. Endpoints that return the analysis schema are marked as such; if you display auth-check results to end users, prefer the iframe embed described in [Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui).

tags.name: auth-check
tags.description: Standalone authorization form analysis. The raw analysis schema (`Analysis`, `AuthCheck`, and related types) changes frequently as we expand document-type coverage, legal requirements, and edge cases. For end-user-facing surfaces, mint an embed token and iframe the read-only view — see [Embedding the auth-check UI](#embedding-the-auth-check-ui).

tags.name: visits
tags.description: Patient visit browse operations. Shares the typed browse syntax described in [Browsing tables](#browsing-tables).

tags.name: procedures
tags.description: Procedure browse operations. Shares the typed browse syntax described in [Browsing tables](#browsing-tables).

tags.name: diagnoses
tags.description: Diagnosis browse operations. Shares the typed browse syntax described in [Browsing tables](#browsing-tables).

tags.name: webhooks
tags.description: Outbound webhooks sent to customer-configured URLs

post.description: Fired whenever a record request is created, its status changes, or a sandbox request auto-completes. Delivered as a POST to `companies.request_status_webhook` with a 10-second timeout.

parameters.name: X-Webhook-Signature
parameters.description: HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of the raw request body, signed with the company's `webhook_secret` (UUID bytes). Consumers must recompute this signature and compare in constant time before trusting the payload.

2XX.description: Consumer acknowledges receipt. Non-2xx responses are logged but not retried.

post.description: Fired when a Veritas claims search completes (or a sandbox patient search auto-completes) and reports visit data for one or more patients. Delivered as a POST to `companies.visits_webhook` with a 10-second timeout.

parameters.name: X-Webhook-Signature
parameters.description: HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of the raw request body, signed with the company's `webhook_secret` (UUID bytes). Consumers must recompute this signature and compare in constant time before trusting the payload.

2XX.description: Consumer acknowledges receipt. Non-2xx responses are logged but not retried.

post.description:
Fired after a note is created on a record request via `POST .../record-requests/{record_request_id}/notes`. Delivered as a POST to `companies.request_note_webhook` with a 10-second timeout. The body is the full updated `RecordRequestOutput` for the request the note was attached to — the same shape returned by `GET .../record-requests/{record_request_id}`.

**Tip:** when creating notes through the API, you can pass an optional `email` field on the request body to attribute the note to a specific Hermes user other than the caller. The email must belong to an existing Hermes user in your company, and the caller must hold the `ImpersonateUser` permission.

parameters.name: X-Webhook-Signature
parameters.description: HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of the raw request body, signed with the company's `webhook_secret` (UUID bytes). Consumers must recompute this signature and compare in constant time before trusting the payload.

2XX.description: Consumer acknowledges receipt. Non-2xx responses are logged but not retried.

post.description: Fired after a note is created on a patient via `POST .../patients/{patient_id}/notes`. Delivered as a POST to `companies.patient_note_webhook` with a 10-second timeout. The body is the full updated `PatientOutput` for the patient the note was attached to — the same shape returned by `GET .../patients/{patient_id}`.

parameters.name: X-Webhook-Signature
parameters.description: HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of the raw request body, signed with the company's `webhook_secret` (UUID bytes). Consumers must recompute this signature and compare in constant time before trusting the payload.

2XX.description: Consumer acknowledges receipt. Non-2xx responses are logged but not retried.
